name: Working Credit
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling organization offering personalized one-on-one counseling and workshops to help people build and maintain prime credit scores.
description_long:
Working Credit is a non-profit organization founded on the principle that improved credit access can transform financial outcomes, particularly for underserved communities disproportionately affected by structural racism. The organization addresses a critical gap in financial access: 28 million Americans are "credit invisible," 57 million have subprime credit scores, and racial disparities mean Black and Hispanic residents are twice as likely to lack credit or have subprime scores, contributing to an 8x wealth gap between white and Black households.

Working Credit delivers personalized credit building through one-on-one counseling combined with group workshops. Their approach focuses on teaching the specific rules and behaviors that drive credit scores—not income-based solutions. Clients work with counselors to establish credit profiles, understand credit mechanics, and develop sustainable financial habits. The organization helps participants achieve concrete goals including home purchases, rental qualification, small business launches, and emergency savings.

What distinguishes Working Credit is their explicit focus on narrowing racial wealth gaps and interrupting structural racism through credit access. They publish transparent impact data: in 2023, clients who started with credit scores increased them by an average of 42 points, while those without scores established prime-range scores. As a non-profit, they reinvest all revenue into their mission rather than selling products or working on commission, eliminating conflicts of interest common in the credit industry.

Working Credit appears genuinely focused on long-term financial resilience rather than quick fixes. However, the website doesn't specify wait times, geographic service areas, availability of services (online/in-person), or detailed program timelines. Their impact metrics are impressive but lack denominator context for some statistics (e.g., what percentage of counseled clients purchased homes). Like all credit counseling, results depend heavily on client engagement and adherence to recommendations.
address: 111 W Washington St Ste 900, Chicago, IL 60602

company_info.headquarters: 111 W Washington St Ste 900, Chicago, IL 60602

pros: Non-profit structure with no commissions or product sales—eliminates conflicts of interest in credit advice
Transparent impact data showing 42-point average credit score increases for clients with existing scores in 2023
Explicit focus on addressing racial wealth gaps and structural racism in credit access
One-on-one personalized counseling combined with group workshops for comprehensive education
Helps clients achieve specific financial goals including home purchases, business launches, and rental qualification
Credit score improvements documented to help establish prime scores even for those previously credit invisible
Lifetime tools and confidence-building approach rather than temporary credit fixes
cons: Website does not specify geographic service areas or whether services are available online or in-person only
No information provided about wait times, program duration, or how long credit improvements typically take
Impact statistics lack full context—percentage of clients who purchased homes is stated without denominator
No details on counselor qualifications, NFCC certification status, or HUD approval mentioned on main pages
Limited information about what happens after counseling ends or long-term success follow-up rates
best_for: People with no credit history or credit-invisible status seeking to establish prime credit for the first time
Black and Hispanic households facing systemic barriers to credit access and wealth building
Individuals in subprime credit situations wanting personalized guidance on sustainable credit improvement
Prospective homebuyers, renters, or small business owners who need credit rehabilitation first
diagnosis: Working Credit is best for people who are credit invisible, have subprime scores, or are building credit for the first time and need personalized guidance—particularly those from communities disproportionately affected by credit discrimination. The main caveat is that results depend entirely on client engagement and adherence, and the website lacks specifics about accessibility (geography, online availability, wait times, program duration) needed to determine if they serve your area or timeline.
services: Credit building workshops and group educational programming
Credit score assessment and baseline establishment
Financial habit and credit management coaching
Guidance on establishing credit profiles for credit-invisible individuals
Home purchase preparation and qualification assistance
Debt and financial obstacle navigation support
Long-term financial resilience and wealth creation guidance
